name: Simple Salmon Chowder
recipeInstructions:
(2 small cans or 1 large can)
1. Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add celery and onion, and sauté until softened and lightly browned.
3. Add potatoes and enough water to cover the potatoes by about an inch.
4. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes,
or until potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork.
5. In a small bowl, mix cornstarch with about 1 cup of water to form
a slurry.
6. Stir the cornstarch mixture into the pot, adding it gradually while
stirring constantly.
7. Increase heat to medium and continue cooking until the chowder
thickens, about 5-10 minutes.
8. Stir in evaporated milk and cooked salmon, reserving some for garnish if desired.
9. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Serve hot, garnished with additional cooked salmon pieces if desired.
Serves 4.
